title: Chapter 7 - Undoing Mistakes (Without Panic)
description: Check your understanding of undo tools like restore, revert, reset, and stash.

questions.prompt: What is `git restore` commonly used for?

options.label: Throw away uncommitted changes in a file

explanations.overview: `git restore` helps you undo changes in your working directory before committing.

byOptionId.a: Incorrect. Uploading is done with `git push`.
byOptionId.b: Correct. It discards local changes to a file.
byOptionId.c: Incorrect. Repos are created with `git init` (or on GitHub).
byOptionId.d: Incorrect. Remotes are managed with `git remote`.

questions.prompt: Why is `git revert` often the safest way to undo a pushed commit?

options.label: It deletes the repository from GitHub
It removes all merge conflicts automatically
It creates a new commit that undoes the change, without rewriting history

explanations.overview: Revert keeps shared history stable by adding an undo commit instead of rewriting commits.

byOptionId.a: Incorrect. Revert does not delete repositories.
byOptionId.b: Incorrect. Revert does not make branches private.
byOptionId.c: Incorrect. Revert does not automatically resolve merge conflicts.
byOptionId.d: Correct. Revert creates a new commit to undo changes without rewriting history.

questions.prompt: Why can `git reset` be risky on a branch other people use?

options.label: It can rewrite history and confuse collaborators

explanations.overview: Reset can move a branch pointer backward, which rewrites the shared story of commits.

byOptionId.a: Correct. Rewriting shared history can cause problems for others.
byOptionId.b: Not quite. Reset does not change your screen.
byOptionId.c: Not quite. GitHub still works; the issue is mismatched history.
byOptionId.d: Incorrect. You can still commit after reset.

options.label: Creates a new branch on GitHub
Temporarily saves uncommitted changes so you can switch tasks

explanations.overview: Stash stores your local work-in-progress changes so your working directory becomes clean.

byOptionId.a: Incorrect. Stash does not create branches.
byOptionId.b: Incorrect. Stash does not delete commits.
byOptionId.d: Incorrect. Staging is done with `git add`.
